images.alt:
Editors’ Note: May 1, 2026
This article was updated after The Times learned that a remark attributed to Pierre Poilievre, the Conservative leader, was in fact an A.I.-generated summary of his views about Canadian politics that A.I. rendered as a quotation. The reporter should have checked the accuracy of what the A.I. tool returned.  The article now accurately quotes from a speech delivered by Mr. Poilievre in April. He said, “My personal opinion is that when a member of Parliament goes back on the word they made to their constituents and switches parties, constituents should be able to petition to throw them out and have a byelection. That would put the people back in charge of our democracy rather than having dirty backroom Liberal deals by Mark Carney determine our destiny.” He did not refer to politicians who changed allegiances as turncoats in that speech.

When the application is compromised, the AI inherits the compromise automatically. CVSS doesn't capture that. My new piece in Dark Reading on why every old vulnerability in an AI-enabled app needs to be reprioritized.

CVE-2026-26144 is a plain XSS in Excel. Two years ago it would have stolen a session cookie. In April it chained with Copilot Agent to silently exfiltrate spreadsheet data with zero clicks, because the trust boundary between Excel and its embedded AI is effectively non-existent.

images.alt:
🎙️ Charaktere & Cast (Japanisch)
»Clarence« von Yōhei Azakami
»Katrina« von Miku Itō

»Saved by the Ice Cold Prince's Embrace« entsteht bei Imagica Infos & Imageworks Studio unter der Regie von Yūsuke Morishita (I Want to Live a Long Life to Dote on My Favorite Stepbrother), die Soundregie übernimmt Yoshiyuki Oguri, die Sound Effects stammen von Hiromune Kurahashi (Otonarium) und die Produktion wird von Stain Lab unterstützt.

📜 Beschreibung 》
Als uneheliche Tochter eines Grafen und einer Dienstmagd kennt Katrina seit ihrer Kindheit kaum Wärme. Nach dem Tod ihrer Mutter wird das Leben im Haus ihres Vaters noch grausamer, denn ihre Stiefmutter und Stiefschwester lassen keine Gelegenheit aus, sie zu demütigen. Als die Stiefschwester wegen ihres schlechten Benehmens an den kalten Nordrand des Reiches geschickt werden soll, trifft die Gräfin eine rücksichtslose Entscheidung: Nicht ihre eigene Tochter, sondern Katrina soll die Strafe antreten. Voller Angst reist sie in den Norden, wo ein angeblich erbarmungsloser Prinz auf sie wartet. Doch hinter der eisigen Fassade begegnet ihr eine Güte, mit der sie nie gerechnet hätte.

A Dietitian’s Advice for a Balanced Post-Run Breakfast

🤖 IA: It's not clickbait ✅
👥 Users: It's not clickbait ✅

#diet 

View full AI summary:

external.description: Karima Hazim, a cook and author, shared a detailed account of her meals throughout a typical day, highlighting both her post-run snack and recipe-testing days in the kitchen. After a seven-kilometre jog, she consumes a banana, a medjool date, and a few walnuts, which dietitian Dr Joanna McMillan notes could be made more nutritionally complete by turning it into a smoothie with Greek yoghurt, oats, berries, and nuts. Throughout the day, Hazim enjoys a Mediterranean-style diet including sardines in a Lebanese bread wrap, roasted cauliflower with cannellini beans, and a variety of snacks like halawet el jibn and dried mango with dark chocolate. McMillan praises the day’s meals for providing healthy fats, fibre, omega-3s, vitamin D, and a diverse range of nutrients beneficial for heart, brain, and gut health. She also cautions against constant sparkling water consumption for dental health and encourages more consistent protein and vegetable intake on recipe-testing days. Overall, the article emphasises small, mindful adjustments to an already balanced diet to improve post-run recovery and general nutrition without compromising enjoyment of food.
external.title: A Dietitian’s Advice for a Balanced Post-Run Breakfast

Canada-India Collaboration Advances Pulse Protein Research to Enhance Food Security

🤖 IA: It's not clickbait ✅
👥 Users: It's not clickbait ✅

#pulseprotein #foodsecurity #canadaindiapartnership 

View full AI summary:

external.description: Canada and India have launched the Canada-India Pulse Protein Centre of Excellence, a collaborative initiative designed to strengthen food security through research and innovation in pulse proteins. Canada, as the world's largest exporter of pulses, meets part of India's growing domestic demand, which is projected to reach 40 million tonnes by 2030. The University of Saskatchewan (USask) will lead the Canadian efforts, partnering with the National Institute of Food Technology Entrepreneurship and Management in India. The centre will support joint research, technology development, graduate training, and knowledge exchange between the two nations. Saskatchewan, producing roughly 90 per cent of Canada's lentils and 87 per cent of its chickpeas, plays a central role in this partnership. USask’s expertise spans plant breeding, processing, nutrition, and food security, positioning it to develop value-added pulse protein products. Researchers like Dr. Michael Nickerson are focusing on improving protein content, digestibility, and nutritional benefits while developing low-cost processing strategies to support food security in both countries. The initiative also emphasizes sustainable agricultural practices and reducing environmental impacts. The collaboration aims to create long-term societal benefits, strengthen international trade ties, and support nutrition programs in India, ultimately contributing to global food security.
external.title: Canada-India Collaboration Advances Pulse Protein Research to Enhance Food Security

The decline of vasectomies among British men and the factors behind it

🤖 IA: It's clickbait ⚠️
👥 Users: It's clickbait ⚠️

#vasectomy #contraception #nhs 

View full AI summary:

external.description: Tim Burrows recounts his personal experience of having a vasectomy in Essex after waiting nearly two years on the NHS list. He reflects on the relatively simple and pain-free procedure, contrasting it with the exaggerated fears often spread online. Research indicates that vasectomy rates in the UK have significantly decreased, from around 30,400 procedures in 2004-05 to just 10,880 in 2015-16, highlighting a 62% drop. Men remain largely absent from contraceptive responsibility, with only 5.3% of contraceptive service users being male. Online platforms amplify rare complications, with anecdotal reports of post-surgical pain and libido issues contributing to widespread misconceptions. Funding cuts and limited access during the austerity years also discouraged men from undergoing the procedure, turning it into a postcode lottery. Burrows points out that these barriers reflect broader societal challenges, including economic uncertainty that affects family planning decisions. Ultimately, while vasectomy is a straightforward and safe procedure, structural, cultural, and informational factors have contributed to its decline among British men, leaving women to bear the primary contraceptive burden.
external.title: The decline of vasectomies among British men and the factors behind it

external.description: The UK Prime Minister, Sir Keir Starmer, has announced plans for the nationalisation of British Steel, specifically targeting the Scunthorpe plant owned by the Chinese firm Jingye. This move aims to safeguard the future of the UK's steel industry, which is considered of huge strategic importance. The plant employs around 3,500 people and produces essential steel for UK rail tracks. Government officials, including Chancellor Rachel Reeves, are aligned on this decision, with legislation reportedly being prepared for inclusion in the King's Speech. The decision comes after previous interventions to prevent the collapse of the plant and reflects broader efforts to secure domestic steel production amid concerns over foreign ownership and industrial stability. Nationalisation would see the state take full control, ending years of uncertainty under Chinese ownership. This policy is part of a wider strategy to bolster British manufacturing and economic resilience. Critics and supporters alike recognise the significance for jobs and national infrastructure, though details on costs and timelines are still emerging. The announcement underscores the Labour government's commitment to protecting key industries and making Britain stronger through direct state involvement in strategic sectors.

external.description: In January, Colorado lawmakers introduced a proposal to make operating systems collect users’ ages and pass them to app developers. The bill, SB26-051, had clearly been designed for commercial platforms like iOS and Android — one of numerous plans to age-gate the internet through users’ devices. It was intended to provide information that would let developers disable age-inappropriate experiences for kids. But as it made the rounds online, Linux laptop maker Carl Richell read the proposal with dismay. Carl Richell is the founder and CEO of Dever-based System76, which also develops the Pop!_OS Linux distribution.

record.text:
Every document tracker (DocSend, Papermark, Pitch) was built for PDF. They convert HTML on upload and lose what made it worth sending.

HTMLRadar serves the HTML as-is. Section dwell via IntersectionObserver. 14 KB tracker. AGPL-3.0.

github.com/htmlradar/htmlradar

PDFs were a pre-LLM artifact: static page count, no real links, embedded media that breaks, no responsive layout.

HTML is everything PDF isn't — and it composes with the way LLMs already output. The format moved. The analytics tooling didn't follow.

The bigger shift behind this: HTML is quietly becoming the default format for new documents.

Specs, briefs, design mocks, reports, dashboards — teams using ChatGPT, Claude, v0, Lovable, Anthropic Artifacts ship them as HTML now. Not PDF. Not Markdown.

GPT Image 2 gana en concepto, pero falla en producción

Un estudio de Contra Labs encontró que el nuevo modelo de imágenes de ChatGPT supera a otros sistemas en pruebas creativas, pero todavía tropieza cuando debe entregar piezas listas para publicarse sin pasar por Photoshop, Illustrator o Figma.…

external.description: Un estudio de Contra Labs encontró que el nuevo modelo de imágenes de ChatGPT supera a otros sistemas en pruebas creativas, pero todavía tropieza cuando debe entregar piezas listas para publicarse sin pasar por Photoshop, Illustrator o Figma. En medio de una conversación cada vez más polarizada sobre las imágenes generadas con inteligencia artificial, un nuevo estudio concluye que GPT Image 2 puede producir buenos conceptos visuales, pero todavía no resuelve de forma confiable el trabajo completo de producción. Contra Labs probó ChatGPT Images 2.0 en 41 sesiones con 7 diseñadores y 6 briefs ficticios de campaña.
external.title: GPT Image 2 gana en concepto, pero falla en producción

La IA que mira píxeles puede no estar viendo el mundo

Un estudio sobre sistemas físicos sugiere que los modelos entrenados en representaciones abstractas pueden capturar mejor las reglas de fenómenos dinámicos que aquellos diseñados para reconstruir imágenes cuadro por cuadro. Durante años, buena…

external.description: Un estudio sobre sistemas físicos sugiere que los modelos entrenados en representaciones abstractas pueden capturar mejor las reglas de fenómenos dinámicos que aquellos diseñados para reconstruir imágenes cuadro por cuadro. Durante años, buena parte de la inteligencia artificial ha aprendido a mirar el mundo como una secuencia de señales que debe predecir el siguiente fotograma, o el siguiente cuadro de video. Esa estrategia ha producido avances enormes, pero también deja abierta una pregunta incómoda: predecir cómo se ve algo no significa necesariamente entender cómo funciona. Esa es una de las ideas centrales de un estudio reciente sobre aprendizaje de representaciones para sistemas físicos espaciotemporales.
external.title: La IA que mira píxeles puede no estar viendo el mundo

Ataque a paquetes de npm y PyPI; TanStack y Mistral aparecen entre los afectados

La campaña, vinculada con Mini Shai-Hulud, no fue una caída de npm, sino un ataque contra la cadena de publicación de software: paquetes aparentemente legítimos habrían distribuido código para robar credenciales de…

external.description: La campaña, vinculada con Mini Shai-Hulud, no fue una caída de npm, sino un ataque contra la cadena de publicación de software: paquetes aparentemente legítimos habrían distribuido código para robar credenciales de desarrolladores. Una nueva campaña de ataque a la cadena de suministro de software encendió alertas entre desarrolladores y equipos de seguridad, luego de que advisories públicos, mantenedores de proyectos y organismos institucionales reportaran paquetes comprometidos en los ecosistemas de npm y PyPI, dos de los registros más usados para instalar dependencias en proyectos de JavaScript y Python.
external.title: Ataque a paquetes de npm y PyPI; TanStack y Mistral aparecen entre los afectados

external.description: Una posible ruptura sacude Silicon Valley y Wall Street. La alianza entre Apple y OpenAI, que cumple ya dos años, se ha deteriorado en las últimas semanas y amenaza con estallar, según fuentes cercanas a las firmas citadas por Bloomberg. record.text: El bloqueo total de Trump deja a Cuba sin una gota de combustible y agrava la crisis económica y social dozz.es/_3dwf3

external.description: Cuba ha asegurado que el país se ha quedado completamente sin diésel ni fueloil que necesita para mantener en funcionamiento sus centrales eléctricas, y ha advertido de que están empezando a surgir disturbios civiles. Esa nueva crisis es la consecuencia del bloqueo energético total impuesto por Donald Trump contra el país, en un intento de hacer caer al Gobierno comunista de la isla. "Hoy estamos sin reservas de combustible, sin ninguna reserva", declaró el ministro de Energía, Vicente de la O Levy, en una rueda de prensa. "No queda absolutamente nada".
external.title: El bloqueo total de Trump deja a Cuba sin una gota de combustible y agrava la crisis económica y social
